messages.content: These risk write-ups share funnel target nodes, which does NOT make them the same risk — a funnel is coarse, so mechanistically distinct risks (a certification clock, a grid-connection queue, a distributor line-review cycle) can all compile to the same node. PARTITION the cluster into groups of TRUE duplicates: same underlying mechanism, same causal story, such that presenting both to a client would read as repetition. Distinct risks come back as singleton groups (absorbs: []). For a genuine duplicate group, pick the best exemplar (sharpest mechanism, best evidence) as keepId and fold the absorbed write-ups' UNIQUE early-warning indicators into it. Over-merging destroys distinct insights — when in doubt, keep them separate.

content: {"rationale":"The first two write-ups (oem-cell-double-squeeze-foreclosure and oem-cell-two-sided-squeeze) are the same risk: identical mechanism — the OEM/integrator cell is squeezed from both sides, brand-spec goes to incumbents, white-label goes to Asian ODMs, entrant sits in the dead zone. Same causal story, same evidence. The second is sharper (quantifies the ~0.08 addressable fraction) so it becomes keepId. The channel-calendar-lockout risk is mechanistically distinct: it's about annual line-card/qualification cycles that a 12-month plan cannot intersect (a timing/calendar mechanism), spanning both OEM and distributor cells — a different causal story than the two-sided squeeze. The framework-foreclosure risk targets a different cell (large-operator direct) and a different mechanism: HQ-level global framework agreements that mean a regional entrant never sees the RFP. Both remain singletons.","groups":[{"keepId":"risk.competitive-foreclosure.oem-cell-two-sided-squeeze","absorbs":["risk.ledger-self-audit.oem-cell-double-squeeze-foreclosure"],"foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"ODM expansion into Europe (local stock, CE/TÜV-certified white-label lines) and distributor line-card additions of Asian PDU lines","where":"Digipower and peer ODM product/price lists; distributor line-card announcements (master distributors adding Asian PDU lines)","threshold":"≥1 major CE distributor adding an ODM white-label PDU line","updates":"increases"}]},{"keepId":"risk.base-rate-analogy.channel-calendar-lockout","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.structure-independence.framework-foreclosure-large-operator-cell","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]}]}
